MW

PhD Candidate, Information School · University of Washington

Marx Wang

My research sits at the intersection of HCI and LLM Alignment, examining relational and affective dynamics in human-AI interaction through mixed methods: qualitative conversation analysis grounded in developmental psychology, computational psycholinguistics/NLP, and mechanistic interpretability using Sparse Autoencoders to identify and steer affect-aligned features in LLM activations.

marxwang@uw.edu

  • Google Scholar
  • GitHub
  • LinkedIn
  • X / Twitter
  • CV

About

My research sits at the intersection of HCI and LLM Alignment, examining relational and affective dynamics in human-AI interaction through mixed methods: qualitative conversation analysis grounded in developmental psychology, computational psycholinguistics/NLP, and mechanistic interpretability using Sparse Autoencoders to identify and steer affect-aligned features in LLM activations.

My work spans both empirical studies of how users engage with conversational agents and technical methods for auditing and modulating the emotional behavior of language models.

Education

Ph.D., Information Science — University of Washington August 2023 – Present
Research Focus: Applied AI, Conversational Agents, Affective Computing
Advisors: Dr. Alexis Hiniker, Dr. Anind K Dey
M.S., Computer Science and Applications — Virginia Tech August 2021 – May 2023
Advisors: Dr. Sang Won Lee (Chair), Dr. Kurt Luther, Dr. Eugenia Rho
Thesis: Understanding How Users Express Their Social Identities through Animated GIF for Public Discourse
GPA: 4.0/4.0
B.S., Computer Science, Data Centric Computing — Virginia Tech August 2018 – May 2021
GPA: 3.98/4.0 · Summa Cum Laude

Research Experience

Research Assistant / Senior Data Scientist Fall 2023 – Present
User Empowerment Lab, University of Washington
  • Developed mechanistic interpretability methods to extract and validate linear "truth directions" from Llama hidden states, implementing claim-boundary token extraction, LDA-based probing, and ghost direction artifact removal.
  • Engineered a mechanical steering framework for Llama 3.2 to disentangle and manipulate causal directions of model sentiment using Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) and Contrastive Activation Addition.
  • Designed computational psycholinguistic approaches and sentiment analysis pipelines to evaluate LLM capacity for emotional attunement across multi-turn conversations.
  • Analyzed 14,000+ conversation threads to develop a taxonomy of how youth use LLMs for the "transition to adulthood."
  • Conducted in-depth qualitative analysis of youth self-disclosure to characterize "AI Botsplaining" failure modes.
  • Investigating how RLHF reward models can inadvertently reinforce maladaptive dependency in anxious users.
  • Collected and analyzed 200k+ in-the-wild ChatGPT interactions for fine-tuning models to detect vulnerable user states.
Research Assistant / Software Engineer Fall 2019 – 2023
EchoLab, Virginia Tech
  • Led a team of 3 to design and implement an ML pipeline that visualizes user emotions through auto-suggesting GIFs in real time, achieving 95.6% accuracy.
  • Built web scraping scripts and collected a dataset of ~1,000,000 GIFs for computational sociolinguistic studies.
  • Designed user studies with real-time collaborative tasks; engineered 6 novel chat systems in MeteorJS.
  • Evaluated the UX of Microsoft HoloLens and developed synchronous web apps for VR studies.
Research Assistant / UX Designer Jan 2020 – 2023
Mind Music Machine Lab, Virginia Tech
  • Designed and implemented EchoFluid, an AI-powered choreography tool using reinforcement learning for real-time dance movement analysis.
  • Designed mapping systems to translate visual features of art to auditory parameters.
Research Assistant / Software Engineer June 2021 – Present
Affective Neurodynamics and Development Lab, Virginia Tech
  • Used ML to forecast children's prosocial behaviors, improving model accuracy from 55% to 85%.
  • Conducted fMRI scan analyses and regression analyses modeling family, neighborhood, and environmental factors.
Research Assistant / Software Engineer June 2021 – 2023
Crowd Intelligence Lab, Virginia Tech
  • Conducted user research on elderly users' privacy concerns and designed visualizations communicating ethical challenges in AI.
  • Designed UI/UX of Civil War Twin, an educational website using AI for historical face matching.

  • Graduate Teaching Assistant, Virginia Tech Spring 2022
    Professionalism in Computing · with Dr. Daniel Dunlap · 200 students

Leadership & Social Impact

Principal, ForYouPage.Org (FYP) 2023 – Present
Youth-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit for mental health and well-being advocacy
  • Built global youth advocacy platform reaching 30,000+ members across 6 continents
  • Mobilized 200+ high-impact youth leaders influencing international policy
  • Established partnerships with 600+ organizations worldwide
  • UN Science Summit UNGA78-80 (2023-2025): Convened 60+ leaders from 40 major organizations
  • Direct advisory engagement with prime ministers, UN leadership, and global policymakers
Associate & Board Member, Ourmedia 2024
501(c)(3) nonprofit for Gen Z leadership and AI education
  • Spearheaded partnerships with major Silicon Valley firms such as IDEO
  • Leading Gen Z advocacy in AI readiness and workforce development
  • Pioneering AI education programs for underserved communities
Raft Crisis Hotline (Jan 2022 – Present) · Mock Trial, Regional Best Attorney (Sept 2021 – Present) · Phi Beta Kappa (May 2021) · Phi Alpha Delta · Active Minds · United Feminist Movement
